Ready · Set · Go
✓ READY
SET
GO
READY since 5:26am Apr 13. Pack go-bag. Avoid riverbanks. SET at 6". GO at 1". Water still rising as of Apr 14.
Scenario
Partial Breach — Overtopping / Erosional FailureEst. peak 20,000–35,000 cfs (Froehlich 2008). Manning's buffer method. +0.5 ft snowmelt saturation correction.
Flood Zones
Breach Analysis
⚠ The Time ProblemIn a partial or full breach, downtown Cheboygan has only 8–25 min after structural failure. IPAWS wireless alerts take 2–5 min to push. Do not wait for an official order if you are in a flood zone and conditions deteriorate.
Peak discharge estimates
Overtopping only
14–20k cfs
Normal max ops
7,640 cfs
Partial breach
20–35k cfs
Full breach
50–100k cfs
Flood wave arrival times
LocationPartialFull
US-23 Bridge (0.5 mi)5–10 min3–6 min
Downtown core (1.5 mi)15–25 min8–15 min
Coast Guard / port (2.5 mi)25–40 min15–25 min
Lake Huron (7 mi)60–90 min35–55 min
Upstream Impacts (Full Breach)
40-mile Inland Waterway systemMullet Lake: est. 3–5 ft drop. Burt Lake: est. 2–4 ft drop. 900,000-acre watershed. Entire Inland Waterway no longer navigable. Ecological damage across connected lake and river system. Recovery timeline: months to years.
Model Notes
Manning's WSE + USGS 3DEP Terrain (pre-computed)Zone widths from Manning's equation (n=0.030 channel, n=0.080 floodplain) + power-law WSE attenuation (γ partial=0.35, full=0.25). Centerline from verified GPS coordinates — edit RCL array in source to adjust. Discharge estimates: Froehlich (2008) + MacDonald & Langridge-Monopolis (1984). Not a certified hydraulic model. For planning guidance only.
Latest Updates
MON APR 13 · OVERNIGHT
Crane removes additional gates. Hydro turbines restarted. Water continues rising despite interventions.
MON APR 13 · 07:00
DNR 7am report: 13.75" below top. WSE 595.2 ft NAVD88.
MON APR 13 · 05:26
READY status reached. 12" below, rising 3"+/day.
SUN APR 12 · 19:00
US-23 bridge raised. Detour via Lincoln Ave Bridge.
FRI APR 10 · 10:00
SEOC activated. Governor declares State of Emergency.
Upstream Watershed
900,000-acre Cheboygan River Basin Toggle the watershed boundary using the 🌊 Watershed button. The entire shaded area drains into the dam — every lake, river, and stream shown is contributing to the current flood event.

Key upstream sources: Mullet Lake, Burt Lake, Black Lake, Indian River, Crooked Lake, Sturgeon River, Maple River.
Emergency Resources
🔔Get Alerts — Be Alert / Smart911 📞Call 211 — Info & Assistance 🏛MSP/EMHSD Official Updates 🚨Emergency: 911
